g.text: SHA-256: The Four Sigma Functions — Uppercase Σ vs Lowercase σ

text.tspan: FATAL BUG: Mixing Σ (uppercase) constants into σ (lowercase) or vice versa
produces plausible-looking 32-bit values with NO error — caught ONLY by NIST vectors

g.text: DEBUGGING: HOW TO CATCH THE SWAP BUG
Applied to working variables a and e inside sha256_compress()
Applied to schedule words W[t-15] and W[t-2] inside sha256_message_schedule()
Uppercase Σ vs Lowercase σ — Structural Comparison
Operations used | ROTR only (3 rotations) | ROTR x2 + SHR x1
Bits destroyed | NONE — full 32-bit entropy | 3 or 10 bits via SHR
Invertible? | YES (pure rotation bijection)| NO (SHR is lossy)
Applied to | Working vars a, e (rich) | Schedule words (sparse)
Naming in FIPS | Σ0, Σ1 (capital sigma) | σ0, σ1 (small sigma)
Design Rationale for SHR in σ functions
Working vars a-h carry FULL 256-bit entropy from previous rounds
→ pure ROTR Σ is safe: no bits need destroying for security
Schedule words W[0..15] start from sparse message (often zeros)
→ SHR in σ makes expansion NON-INVERTIBLE
→ Cannot recover original W[t] from σ(W[t]) — one-way mixing
→ Prevents attacker from solving schedule backwards to find collision
SHR is deliberate irreversibility, not an accident of bit arithmetic
All Four Sigma Functions — Constants at a Glance
Function | Where Used | Constant Set | Op Types
Σ0(a) | T2 in compress | 2, 13, 22 | ROTR ROTR ROTR
Σ1(e) | T1 in compress | 6, 11, 25 | ROTR ROTR ROTR
σ0(x) | W[t-15] expand | 7, 18, 3 | ROTR ROTR SHR
σ1(x) | W[t-2] expand | 17, 19, 10 | ROTR ROTR SHR
MEMORY | Σ = even spacing | 2-13-22 gaps | 11 then 9
AID: | σ = odd spacing | 7-18 then 3 | 11 then -15
W[16..63] wrong, W[0..15] OK| σ constants swapped | Check (7,18,3) and (17,19,10)
T1 wrong at round 0 | Σ1 constants wrong | Must be (6,11,25) not (17,19,10)
T2 wrong at round 0 | Σ0 constants wrong | Must be (2,13,22) not (7,18,3)
All rounds wrong after R0 | Σ0 vs Σ1 swapped | Σ0→a in T2, Σ1→e in T1
Schedule ok, compress wrong | Used SIGMA_LOWER in T1/T2| Replace with SIGMA_UPPER macros
Values plausible but wrong | ANY sigma swap | Compare ALL 64 W[t] to NIST PDF

text.tspan: /* used in T1 = h + SIGMA_UPPER_1(e) + CH(e,f,g) + K[t] + W[t] */
/* used in T1 = h + SIGMA_UPPER_1(e) + CH(e,f,g) + K[t] + W[t] */

g.text: Σ ≠ σ — different constants, different ops
